text: One absorbs light, the other creates its own. Can you guess which is which? Biofluorescence allows organisms to absorb light and re-emit it, often in vibrant colors. On the flip side, bioluminescence is a chemical reaction producing light without needing external sources. Think jellyfish and fireflies—bioluminescent marvels that light up the dark. Both adaptations are vital for survival, attracting mates or evading predators. Now you know the difference! Want to learn more about the ocean's secrets? Like and follow!
